title: Fajita Chicken Casserole (High-Protein Recipe) | Eati
introduction: Fajita Chicken Casserole is a protein-forward baked dinner with juicy chicken, colorful peppers, and bold fajita seasoning. It is perfect for weeknight cooking and batch-friendly meal prep.

sections.heading: Ingredients
sections.content:
- Chicken
- Bell peppers
- Onion
- Fajita spices (cumin, chili powder, paprika)
- Garlic (optional)
- Mozzarella (or a similar cheese)
- Optional: black beans
- Salt, pepper, and olive oil

sections.heading: Instructions
sections.content:
1. Preheat oven to 375F (190C).
2. Season and cook chicken briefly in a skillet to develop flavor.
3. Saute peppers and onion until slightly tender.
4. Layer chicken, peppers/onion, and cheese in a baking dish; bake until bubbly.
5. Serve and log your serving size in Eati for accurate macros.

sections.heading: Calories, Fat, and Protein (Estimated)
sections.content:
Calories: 363 kcal per serving (estimated)
Fat: 13g
Protein: 49g

Tip: keep toppings light to preserve a lower-fat macro profile.

sections.heading: Why This Fajita Casserole Is an Elite Fat-Loss Meal
sections.content:
Let's put the numbers in perspective. At 363 calories with 49g of protein and only 13g of fat, this casserole is one of the most nutritionally efficient fat-loss meals you can make:

• 13g protein per 100 calories — top 5% of all recipes for protein density.
• Only 13g fat — makes room for other fats in your day.
• High in vitamin C, B6, and fiber — thanks to the peppers and onions.
• Gluten-free friendly — naturally, without any modifications.
• Low-carb — approximately 10g net carbs per serving.

Comparison to traditional fajitas at restaurants:
• Chili's chicken fajitas with tortillas, rice, beans: 1,100–1,400 calories.
• Chipotle chicken bowl with similar fillings: 700–900 calories.
• This casserole: 363 calories.

You can eat this casserole twice and still come in under a single restaurant fajita meal. For comparison with other high-protein options, see [30 best high-protein recipes](/blog/30-best-high-protein-recipes). Use a protein calculator to determine your daily target.

sections.heading: Homemade Fajita Seasoning (Better Than Store-Bought)
sections.content:
Commercial fajita seasoning often contains fillers, MSG, excessive salt, and sometimes sugar. Making your own takes 2 minutes and tastes dramatically better.

Fajita seasoning blend (enough for 4 batches):
• 2 tbsp chili powder
• 1 tbsp smoked paprika
• 1 tbsp cumin
• 1 tbsp garlic powder
• 2 tsp onion powder
• 1 tsp oregano
• 1 tsp black pepper
• 1 tsp salt
• 1/2 tsp cayenne (optional, for heat)

Mix in a small jar. Use 1–2 tablespoons per pound of chicken.

Why homemade wins:
• No anti-caking agents (silicon dioxide, etc.)
• Control sodium — commercial blends often have 500–800mg per teaspoon.
• Fresher flavor — spices lose potency after 6 months; use within 3 months.
• Cheaper — about $0.10 per batch vs $1 for packets.

Flavor boosters to add at serving:
• Fresh lime juice (huge flavor upgrade)
• Fresh cilantro
• A pinch of chipotle powder for smokiness

Adjust salt based on how salty your other ingredients are (cheese, canned beans if you add them).

sections.heading: Making It More or Less Calorie-Dense
sections.content:
The beauty of this base recipe is how flexibly it scales for different goals.

To make it lower calorie (–60 per serving):
• Use part-skim mozzarella (saves 40 cal per oz).
• Skip the added oil; brown chicken in a non-stick pan.
• Omit optional black beans or keep them at 1/4 cup portion.
• Final: ~300 cal, 48g protein per serving.

To make it more calorie-balanced (+150 per serving):
• Add 1/2 cup black beans per serving (adds 110 cal, 7g protein, 20g carbs).
• Top with 1/4 cup Greek yogurt (30 cal, 5g protein).
• Final: ~530 cal, 55g protein per serving — great for muscle building or maintenance.

To make it a burrito bowl (+250 per serving):
• Serve over 1/2 cup brown rice or cilantro-lime rice (+100 cal).
• Add 1/4 avocado (+80 cal).
• Top with pico de gallo (+15 cal) and extra lime.
• Final: ~620 cal, 52g protein per serving.

For a low-carb alternative:
• Omit all grains and beans.
• Serve over cauliflower rice (~30 cal per cup).
• Top with 1/4 avocado and a dollop of sour cream.
• Final: ~440 cal, 50g protein, 12g net carbs per serving.

sections.heading: What to Serve With Fajita Chicken Casserole
sections.content:
The casserole is flexible enough to serve in many formats:

Mexican-inspired sides:
• Cilantro lime rice (1/2 cup, ~110 cal)
• Cauliflower cilantro rice (1 cup, ~40 cal)
• Refried beans (1/2 cup, ~120 cal)
• Black bean salad with corn and lime (~150 cal per cup)
• Mexican street corn (elote) (~150 cal per serving)

Low-calorie toppings:
• Pico de gallo (15 cal per 1/4 cup)
• Diced onions and cilantro
• Lime wedges
• Pickled jalapeños (5 cal)
• Hot sauce (0 cal)
• Nonfat Greek yogurt instead of sour cream (saves 40 cal)

Moderate-calorie toppings:
• 1/4 avocado (~80 cal)
• 2 tbsp guacamole (~50 cal)
• 2 tbsp reduced-fat sour cream (~50 cal)
• 1 tbsp shredded cheese (~25 cal)

Tortilla options:
• Corn tortillas (2, small): ~100 cal
• Whole-wheat tortilla (1, small): ~80 cal
• Low-carb tortilla (1): ~50 cal
• Skip the tortilla entirely — save 100+ cal

Balanced Mexican plate (580 cal, 55g protein): 1 serving casserole + 1/2 cup cilantro lime rice + 1/4 avocado + pico de gallo + lime wedge. See [high-protein low-calorie foods](/blog/high-protein-low-calorie-foods) for more protein-packed ideas.

sections.heading: Meal Prep & Make-Ahead Tips
sections.content:
Casseroles are perfect for batch cooking because they hold up well and reheat evenly.

Full-week prep strategy:
1. Make a 9×13 casserole on Sunday (serves 6).
2. Cool completely before portioning.
3. Divide into 6 containers with different sides:
   • Days 1–2: Rice and salsa.
   • Days 3–4: Cauliflower rice and avocado.
   • Days 5–6: Wrapped in a tortilla with extra cheese.

Storage:
• Fridge: 4 days in airtight glass containers.
• Freezer: 2 months. Wrap tightly to prevent freezer burn.

Reheating:
• Oven (best for large portions): 350°F, covered, for 15 min. Uncover for last 3 min to re-crisp cheese.
• Microwave: 2 min covered, stir, 45 more seconds.

Make-ahead tip: Assemble the entire casserole (chicken, peppers, cheese) in a baking dish, cover, and refrigerate up to 24 hours in advance. Bake for 30 min when you're ready (add 5 min to cook time from cold).

Freezer meal prep:
Assemble but don't bake. Wrap the dish tightly in foil and freeze for up to 2 months. Thaw overnight in the fridge, then bake as directed. For more efficient tracking across the week, see [how to track calories correctly](/blog/how-to-track-calories-correctly).

conclusion: A simple casserole that makes it easy to eat more protein without feeling like you are sacrificing flavor. Track it in Eati and repeat what works.
metaTitle: Fajita Chicken Casserole: 49g Protein in 363 Calories (Low-Carb & Gluten-Free)
metaDescription: This high-protein fajita chicken casserole delivers 49g protein in 363 calories per serving. Homemade fajita seasoning, side pairings, and make-ahead meal prep strategies included.

faqs.question: How many calories in fajita chicken casserole?
faqs.answer: Approximately 363 calories per serving with 49g of protein and 13g of fat. Adding black beans (+110 cal), rice (+100 cal), or avocado (+80 cal) adjusts the total; the base casserole is one of the most protein-dense fat-loss meals you can make.

faqs.question: Is this fajita casserole low carb?
faqs.answer: Yes. The base recipe (chicken, peppers, onion, cheese, spices) is approximately 10g net carbs per serving, making it low-carb and keto-friendly. Adding black beans or rice pushes it out of keto but keeps it reasonable for general low-carb diets.

faqs.question: Can I make fajita casserole ahead of time?
faqs.answer: Yes. Assemble the full dish up to 24 hours in advance, cover, and refrigerate. Add 5 minutes to the bake time when cooking from cold. For longer storage, assemble and freeze uncooked for up to 2 months; thaw overnight before baking.

faqs.question: What protein can I use besides chicken?
faqs.answer: Any lean protein works: skirt steak (fajita classic), shrimp (add in last 10 min), ground turkey, pork tenderloin, or firm tofu. Shrimp fajita casserole is especially popular — cut the cook time by 10 minutes and add shrimp during the last 5 minutes.

faqs.question: How do I make fajita casserole spicier?
faqs.answer: Add 1/2 tsp chipotle powder to your seasoning, or include 1 diced jalapeño with the peppers. For extra heat, top finished portions with sliced fresh jalapeños, hot sauce, or pickled jalapeños. Poblano peppers also add mild heat with great flavor.
